name: Pork with Fermented Bean Curd
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Boneless pork (loin or butt) in 1-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring the liquids to a boil.
2. Add the pork, garlic, and soy sauce.
3. Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes, skimming any foam that forms occasionally.
4. Slice the scallions into 1-inch pieces and add them.
5. Add the fermented bean curd and mash it around until fully incorporated in the sauce.
6. Add sherry (if using less bean curd, use more sherry; if using more bean curd, use less sherry).
7. Continue to simmer for an additional 15 to 30 minutes until the pork is tender.
8. You may add a slice or two of ginger with the garlic, but tradition dictates this step was not always followed.
9. The dish should have visible fat and a thin sauce that slightly curdles. For western presentation, consider serving it with stir-fried green vegetables (spinach was commonly used).
10. Do not use totally lean meat; the result will be stringy and tasteless. Traditional preparation uses butt or fresh bacon, but fattier cuts of loin are also acceptable after removing non-stir-fryable parts.
